jobs.prompt: Create a clean academic paper figure explaining the shared scoring foundation for two RehearFit subject-selection strategies. Show Inside train pool only, then per-subject median of 12-band LTASS row targets, then z-score normalization across subjects. From the normalized 12-D subject vector, branch into two scalar scores: distance_to_center = L2 norm of normalized vector, and high_freq_score = mean of high-frequency components 6K to 12.5K. Use a scatter plot metaphor where each dot is a subject, center distance is radial distance, high-frequency score is color from low to high. Add short labels only: subject vector, z-score, center distance, high-frequency score, no Outside data. White background, vector schematic, teal blue orange accents, conference-paper style, 16:9.

Create a clean academic paper figure focusing only on the RehearFit 5 percent highfreq_quantile subject-selection strategy. Show subjects sorted along a horizontal high_freq_score axis from low to high. Split the sorted line into several quantile bins. Inside each bin, show candidate dots ranked by distance_to_center and highlight one center-near selected subject. Then show selected subjects from all bins flowing into the training set, with all rows from selected subjects retained. Key visual message: preserve high-frequency coverage even when only a few subjects are labeled, while avoiding extreme outliers inside each bin. Use short labels: sort by high_freq_score, split into quantiles, choose center-near candidate, keep all rows. Minimal text, white background, 16:9, paper schematic.

Create a clean academic paper figure focusing only on the RehearFit 10 percent balanced_center subject-selection strategy. Show a 2-D subject distribution scatter plot with a dense center and high-frequency low and high tails. Highlight about 70 percent of selected subjects from the distribution center using distance_to_center, then highlight the remaining subjects from the low and high high_freq_score tails. Show these two streams merging into the training set. Key visual message: most labels represent the common population, while a small tail budget protects high-frequency edge cases. Use short labels: center pool, 70 percent core, high-frequency tails, 30 percent tail budget, selected subjects, keep all rows. White background, sparse vector figure, teal blue orange accents, 16:9.
